dn20:1.0.1: Thus I have heard:at one time the Gracious One was dwelling amongst the Sakyans in the Great Wood near Kapilavatthu with a great Saṅgha of monks consisting of five hundred monks, all of them Worthy Ones, and almost all of the gods from the ten world-elementshad assembled to see the Gracious One and the Saṅgha of monks.
dn20:1.0.2: "There is a great convention in the wood,and a host of gods have come,we have come to this Dhamma conventionto see the Saṅgha, who are undefeated."
dn20:1.0.3: Then another god recited this verse in the presence of the Gracious One:
dn20:1.0.5: Then another god recited this verse in the presence of the Gracious One:
dn20:1.0.7: Then another god recited this verse in the presence of the Gracious One:
dn20:1.1.9: Then the Gracious One addressed the monks, saying: "Monks, almost all of the gods from the ten world-elements have assembled to see the Realised One and the Saṅgha of monks. Monks, those who in the past were Worthy Ones, Perfect Sambuddhas, for those Gracious Ones also at least as many gods have assembled as for me at the present time. Monks, those who in the future will be Worthy Ones, Perfect Sambuddhas, for those Gracious Ones also at least as many gods will assemble as for me at the present time.
dn20:1.1.10: Monks, I will declare the names of the hosts of gods, monks, I will proclaim the names of the hosts of gods, monks, I will reveal the names of the hosts of gods. Listen to it, apply your minds well, and I will speak."
dn20:1.1.11: "I will repeat a famous verse(aSiloka)wherever the gods of the Earth live:There aremonkswho live in a hill cave,who are resolute, composed,
dn20:1.1.12: who are like lions crouching,who have overcomeallhorror,with minds that are cleansed, and purified,which are clear, and undisturbed".
dn20:1.1.13: He knew there were more than five hundredin the wood near Kapilavatthulike this,therefore the Teacher addressedthose disciples who delight in the teaching:
dn20:1.1.14: "A host of gods have drawn nearto us,you should know who they are, monks!"Thenthose monksbecame ardent,after hearing the Buddha’s teaching,
dn20:1.1.15: and knowledge was manifest to them,seeing those non-human beings—and some of them saw a hundred,a thousand, or seventythousand.
dn20:1.1.16: Some of them saw a hundred thousandof those non-human beings,and some saw an endless numberspread out in every direction.
dn20:1.1.17: Having deep knowledge of all thatthe Visionary desired to speak,therefore the Teacher addressedthose disciples who delight in the teaching:
dn20:1.1.18: "A host of gods have drawn nearto us,you should know who they are, monks!I will proclaim theirnamesto youwith lyrics in regular order.
dn20:1.2.19: There are seven thousand yakkhas,Earth gods from Kapilavatthu,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.2.20: From Hemavant there are six thousandyakkhas, diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.2.21: From Sātāgira there are three thousandyakkhas, diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.2.22: Like this there are sixteen thousandyakkhas, diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.2.23: From Vessāmitta there are five hundredyakkhas, diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.2.24: From Rājagaha there is Kumbhira,who is settled on Vepulla,and more than a hundred thousandyakkhas who gather round him,From Rājagaha there is Kumbhirahe also came to the gathering in the wood.
dn20:1.3.25: The Easterly direction, the kingDhataraṭṭharules over that,he is master of the Gandhabbas,he is a resplendent Great King,
dn20:1.3.26: also there are his many sons,Inda by name, ones of great strength,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.3.27: The Southerly direction, the kingVirūḷha rules over that,he is master of the Kumbhaṇḍas,he is a resplendent Great King,
dn20:1.3.28: also there are his many sons,Inda by name, ones of great strength,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.3.29: The Westerly direction, the kingVirūpakkha rules over that,he is master of the nāgas,he is a resplendent Great King,
dn20:1.3.30: also there are his many sons,Inda by name, ones of great strength,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.3.31: The Northerly direction, the kingKuvera rules over that,he is master of the yakkhas,he is a resplendent Great King,
dn20:1.3.32: also there are his many sons,Inda by name, ones of great strength,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.3.33: To the East there is Dhataraṭṭha,in the South there is Virūḷhaka,in the West there is Virūpakkha,to the North there is Kuvera.
dn20:1.3.34: These are the Four Great Kings,from all sides, the four directions,they stood blazing brilliantlyin the wood near Kapilavatthu.
dn20:1.3.35: Their deceitful servants came,who are fraudulent and treacherous:the deceitful Kuteṇḍu, Veteṇḍu,Viṭucca, and Viṭuḍa,
dn20:1.3.36: Candana and Kāmaseṭṭha,Kinnughaṇḍu and Nighaṇḍu,Panāda and Opamañña,and Mātali, the gods' charioteer,
dn20:1.3.37: The gandhabbas Citta and Sena,the kings Nala and Janesabhacame, and also Pañcasikha,Timbaru, and Suriyavaccasā.
dn20:1.3.38: These and also other kings,and gandhabbas along with their kings,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.4.39: Then came Nāgas from Nābhasa,and from Vesāli, and the Tacchakas,the Kambalas, and Assataras came,andnāgasfrom Pāyāga with their kin.
dn20:1.4.40: The Yāmuna, and the Dhataraṭṭha nāgas came,resplendent ones,and Erāvaṇa, the great nāga,he also came to the gathering in the wood.
dn20:1.4.41: Those who carry away the nāga kings by force—the divine, twice-born,winged ones, with clear vision—through the air theycame andarrived in the wood,the Citras and Supaṇṇas, such are their names.
dn20:1.4.42: But at that time the nāga kings were without fear,for the Buddha made them safe from the supaṇṇas.Calling onone anotherwith gentle words,the nāgas and supaṇṇas took refuge in the Buddha.
dn20:1.5.43: Defeated by Vajirahattha,the asuras live in the ocean,they are brothers of Vāsava,having psychic power, resplendent,
dn20:1.5.44: the very fearful Kālakañjas,the Dānaveghasa asuras,Vepacitti and Sucitti,Pahārāda, together with Namuci,
dn20:1.5.45: and a hundred of Bali's sons,all of them named after Veroca.Having armed themselves, Bali's armycame to lucky Rāhuand said:"Now is the time, reverend Sir,for the gathering of the monks in the wood."
dn20:1.6.46: The Water gods, and the Earth gods,the Fire, and the Windgods allcame there,the Varuṇa, and Vāruṇa gods,Soma followed by Yasa,
dn20:1.6.47: and a host of Friendly and Compassionate gods came, resplendent ones.All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,
dn20:1.6.48: who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent.Rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.49: The Veṇhuand Sahali and Asama gods, and the two Yama godscame.The gods who depend on the Moon came,with the Moon in front of them.
dn20:1.6.50: The gods who depend on the Sun came,with the Sun in front of them,with the stars in front of them,also the foolish Rain Cloudgodscame.
dn20:1.6.51: Sakka also came,who is calledVāsava, the best of the Vasū, and Purindada.All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,
dn20:1.6.52: who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.53: Then came the Sahabhu gods,blazing forth, like the crest of a fire,and the Ariṭṭhakas and Rojasand the splendid Ummapupphagods.
dn20:1.6.54: The Varuṇas and Sahadhammas,the Accutas and Anejakas,the Sūleyyas and Rucirasallcame;the Vāsavanesigodscame.
dn20:1.6.55: All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.56: The Samānas, Mahāsamānas,Mānusas, Mānusuttamas,and the Khiḍḍāpadūsikas came;the Manopadūsikas came.
dn20:1.6.57: Then the Hari gods came,and thoseknown asthe Lohitavāsī.The Pāragas, and Mahāpāragas came,gods who are resplendent.
dn20:1.6.58: All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.59: The Sukkas, Karumhas, and Aruṇas came, with the Veghanasas.The Vicakkhaṇa gods came with the Odātagayhas in front.
dn20:1.6.60: The Sadāmattas, Hāragajas,and the resplendent Missakas.Pajjunna came thundering,he who pours down rain inalldirections.
dn20:1.6.61: All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.62: The Khemiyas, Tusitas, Yāmas,and resplendent Kaṭṭhakascame.The Lambītakas, Lāmaseṭṭhas,those called the Jotis, and Āsavas,
dn20:1.6.63: and the Nimmāṇaratis came;then came the Paranimittas.All of these ten hosts, ten kindsof gods,who are diverse in colour,
dn20:1.6.64: who have psychic power, who are bright,beautiful, and resplendent,rejoicing they have drawn nearthe gathering of monks in the wood.
dn20:1.6.65: All of these sixty hosts of gods,who are diverse in colour,in conformity with their names came;these along with others similarthinking:
dn20:1.6.66: "We shall see the castelessSaṅgha, unhindered,flood-crossers, pollution-free,and the nāga, who is beyond the flood,who, like the Moon, has overcome the darkness.
dn20:1.7.67: Subrahmā and Paramatta,along with the sons of the powerful onecame.Sanankumāra and Tissaalso came to the gathering in the wood.
dn20:1.7.68: In the thousand brahma worldsa Great Brahma has arisen,a bright one, who stands out,whose awesome body is resplendent.
dn20:1.7.69: The ten Issarābrahmāscame,who individually wield power,and in the midst of them cameHārita with his entourage.
dn20:1.8.70: Now when they had all drawn near,Inda with the gods and the brahmās,Māra's army also drew near:behold the foolishness of the Dark One!
dn20:1.8.71: "Come now, seize them, and bind them"he said,"let them be bound by passion,surround them on all sides,do not let anyone of them go free!"
dn20:1.8.72: Thus in that place the great army leadersent forth his dark army,after striking the ground with his hand,and making a fearful noise,
dn20:1.8.73: like a storm cloud shedding rain,thundering, with flashes of lightning—but then he retreated,angry, and unable to control himself.
dn20:1.8.74: Knowing all thatwas happeningthe Visionary desired to speak,therefore the Teacher addressedthose disciples who delight in the teaching:
dn20:1.8.75: "Māra's army has drawn nearto us,you should know who they are, monks!"Thenthose monksbecame ardent, after hearing the Buddha’s teaching.They withdrew from the passionlessmonks, not one of their hairs was shaken!
dn20:1.9.76: "They are all victorious in battle,beyond fear, and resplendent,those disciples, famous among men, along withallbeings, are glad."
